evidence: Rendered PDF page 1 shows caption with 'and' positioned between 'Defendant and Appellee' and 'State of North Dakota', not between 'Gino Jose' and 'Defendant and Appellee'. The correct logical structure per PDF is: Kim Goldade-Jose nka Kim Austin (Plaintiff and Appellant) v. Gino Jose (Defendant and Appellee) AND State of North Dakota (Statutory Real Party in Interest).

source: /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2022/2022ND27.pdf pages 3-4 (PDF line 192: page number '4' inserted mid-line between columns)
evidence: PDF source shows page number '4' on its own line (line 192) between 'under the' (end of page 3) and 'influence of alcohol' (beginning of page 4). During text extraction and ingestion, this page number was incorrectly merged into the running text as 'under the 4 influence'. The correct text per the printed opinion is 'under the influence of alcohol'. Confirmed in PDF by visual inspection: paragraph begins at line 188 'follow after a person has tested over the legal limit for driving under the', continues at line 192 with isolated page number '4', then resumes at line 193 'influence of alcohol'.

note: Logan v. Bush citation: DB incorrectly shows '621 N.W.2d 314' while authoritative PDF source shows '619 N.W.2d 501'. Volume and page number both incorrect in DB.

old_exact: do not apply (citing N.D.R.Ev. 1101(d)(3)(C)). to preliminary hearings in criminal cases. Id.
new_exact: do not apply to preliminary hearings in criminal cases. Id. (citing N.D.R.Ev. 1101(d)(3)(C)).
note: Citation parenthetical misplaced within sentence in DB. The parenthetical '(citing N.D.R.Ev. 1101(d)(3)(C)).' appears mid-sentence after 'do not apply' in the DB, creating a sentence fragment 'to preliminary hearings...' Following the authoritative PDF, the citation should follow the complete dependent clause 'to preliminary hearings in criminal cases' and cite only to the rule via the parenthetical after 'Id.'

evidence: The DB text contains 'Srender' (one occurrence at line 499), which is an OCR misread of the letter 'S' being prepended to 'render'. The authoritative PDF source clearly shows 'render' in the same position. This is a straightforward OCR character error where a stray mark or ligature was misread as the letter 'S'.
note: Image-verified via mutool render of PDF page 12 showing 'Statutes and rules are to be construed in a way which does not render them useless' with clear 'render' (not 'Srender')

old_exact: which of postconviction counsel. An applicant may not claim constitutionally ineffective assistance of postconviction counsel in proceedings under this chapter. ineffective assistance allege
new_exact: which allege ineffective assistance of postconviction counsel. An applicant may not claim constitutionally ineffective assistance of postconviction counsel in proceedings under this chapter.
source: PDF 2021ND206.pdf shows continuous text in statute subsection 2 of N.D.C.C. § 29-32.1-09; DB has text fragments scattered across lines with phrase 'allege ineffective assistance of' misplaced and duplicated 'ineffective assistance' at end of subsection
evidence: PDF lines 78-82: '2. The court, on its own motion, may dismiss any grounds of an application which allege ineffective assistance of postconviction counsel. An applicant may not claim constitutionally ineffective assistance of postconviction counsel in proceedings under this chapter.' DB has the same passage but with critical phrase inverted: 'which of postconviction counsel' followed later by orphaned 'ineffective assistance allege'. This is a classic OCR/ingestion fragment error where text was split across page columns and reassembled out of order.
note: This error is in the reproduced statute text within [¶3]. The corrupt form reads as: 'application which of postconviction counsel. An applicant may not claim constitutionally ineffective assistance of postconviction counsel in proceedings under this chapter. ineffective assistance allege 3. The court may grant...' The correct form restores proper statutory language.

PDF shows section heading 'III' appearing BEFORE paragraph 13 (with blank lines in between), while DB has 'III' appearing AFTER paragraph 13. Line-by-line examination confirms PDF structure: '...appeal was untimely.

[¶13] Because we are without jurisdiction...' vs DB: '...appeal was untimely.

[¶13] Because we are without jurisdiction...

III'. This is a heading sequence inversion error in the database.

The DB text at [¶12] is missing the authority citation ', N.D.C.C.,' that appears in the authoritative PDF. This is a standard ingestion defect where the statute reference was split and partially lost. The PDF clearly shows 'Section 14-09-06.6(6)(b), N.D.C.C., requires' while the DB only has 'Section 14-09-06.6(6)(b) requires'. Paragraph [¶9] contains the same pattern correctly: 'Section 14-09-06.6(6)(a), N.D.C.C., requires' in both versions, confirming this is the court's standard format.
note: This is a missing text error at the statute/authority level - the N.D.C.C. citation abbreviation that accompanies the statute reference was dropped during ingestion.

new_exact: Unjust enrichment requires: (1) an enrichment; (2) an impoverishment; (3) a connection between the enrichment and the impoverishment; (4) an absence of justification for the enrichment and impoverishment; and (5) an absence of remedy provided by law.
source: PDF pdftotext /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2021/2021ND171.pdf - lines 406-412: numbered list in [¶27]
note: OCR/ingestion defect: the numbered list elements for the unjust enrichment doctrine are completely reordered. DB begins with '(2) an Unjust enrichment requires: impoverishment' then jumps to (3), (4), then (1), then (5). Source PDF shows proper order: (1) enrichment; (2) impoverishment; (3) connection; (4) absence of justification; (5) absence of remedy. The phrase 'and impoverishment' also repeats incorrectly in the DB and should appear once as 'and impoverishment' after (4).
